explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: The FirstEnergy 2024 Annual Report (10-K) describes the approval and implementation of a grid modernization plan. This plan includes the use of smart meters and automation technology.

relevant_sources.summary: The FirstEnergy 2024 Annual Report (10-K), an SEC filing, provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This report supports governance by describing the approval and implementation of a grid modernization plan, which includes the use of smart meters and automation technology, demonstrating oversight and strategic execution of automated systems.
relevant_sources.title: FirstEnergy 2024 Annual Report (10-K)

oversight.findings: The company describes the implementation of a 'human-in-the-loop' pathway. This pathway includes feedback collection and model retraining performed by security analysts.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post provides evidence for the **oversight** pillar of responsible AI. It details the implementation of a "human-in-the-loop" pathway and describes feedback collection and model retraining by security analysts, demonstrating operational human oversight in the AI system's development and deployment.
relevant_sources.title: Building a Holistic Risk Profile: Near Real-Time Approach to Insider Threat Detection

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: FirstEnergy's published materials document operational practices for governance, including the approval and implementation of a grid modernization plan, and policy-level practices for oversight, such as a 'human-in-the-loop' pathway, addressing 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Additionally, oversight policies describe feedback collection by security analysts. Based on a review of 12 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
